mnemonics: Every Text Hides A Story: Remember the basic features of text-based data hiding by associating them with the acronym ETHAS (Encoding methods, Text features, Human imperceptibility, Algorithms, Steganography).
keyPoints: Steganography is the practice of concealing a message within another medium.
Basic features include using whitespace (spaces, tabs), non-printable characters, and slight formatting changes.
The goal is to alter the carrier text in ways that are imperceptible to a human reader but can be detected by a specific algorithm.
commonMistakes: Using overly complex or obvious patterns that are easily detected, defeating the purpose of hiding.
Altering the text in a way that changes its semantic meaning or looks unnatural to a human, raising suspicion.
